edges.description: Impaired CLN6/EGRESS-mediated ER export of lysosomal enzymes is expected to diminish lysosomal enzyme delivery and promote the lysosomal storage pathology of CLN6-related Kufs disease.
intermediate_mechanisms: inefficient ER export of lysosomal enzymes
edges.source: CLN6 EGRESS Complex Lysosomal Enzyme Trafficking Defect

edges.description: Adult NCL lysosomal storage is directly observable as autofluorescent ceroid/lipopigment storage material in neural tissue.
Lysosomal lipopigment storage contributes to the progressive neurodegeneration that drives the clinical phenotype of adult NCL.
DNAJC5/CSPalpha depletion leads to lysosomal accumulation of misfolded proteolysis-resistant proteins as characteristic ceroid deposits in neurons.
Cathepsin F loss-of-function is proposed to impair lysosomal proteolysis and drive ceroid lipopigment storage in Type B Kufs disease.
Ongoing neurodegeneration produces progressive dementia, an invariable feature of adult NCL.
Neurodegeneration drives myoclonic seizures in Type A Kufs disease.
Neurodegeneration produces progressive ataxia, the most prominent motor feature of CLN6-related Kufs disease.
The progressive myoclonus epilepsy branch of CLN6-related Type A Kufs disease includes tonic-clonic seizures.
Progressive neuronal loss produces cerebral and cerebellar atrophy on neuroimaging.
The Type A epilepsy phenotype includes high EEG photosensitivity.
Increased autofluorescent storage material reports the core lysosomal lipopigment accumulation node.
Proteolysis-resistant neuronal ceroid deposits report the lysosomal protein-aggregation consequence of DNAJC5/CSPalpha dysfunction.
These deposits are a DNAJC5-related manifestation of the broader adult NCL lysosomal lipopigment storage node.
Decreased cathepsin F cysteine protease function reports the CTSF-driven lysosomal proteolysis defect.
edges.target: Cathepsin F lysosomal cysteine protease activity

edges.description: Lower lysosomal enzyme levels report impaired CLN6-dependent ER-to-Golgi lysosomal-enzyme transfer.
edges.source: CLN6 EGRESS Complex Lysosomal Enzyme Trafficking Defect
edges.target: Lysosomal enzyme levels at the lysosome
CLN6 EGRESS Complex Lysosomal Enzyme Trafficking Defect

nodes.description: Antiseizure medications are used to manage progressive myoclonus epilepsy in Type A Kufs disease, although myoclonic seizures are often debilitating despite treatment.

evidence.reference_title: Kufs disease due to mutation of CLN6: clinical, pathological and molecular genetic features.
evidence.snippet: The typical presentation was of progressive myoclonus epilepsy with debilitating myoclonic seizures and relatively infrequent tonic-clonic seizures.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in DNAJC5, encoding cysteine-string protein alpha, cause autosomal-dominant adult-onset neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis.
evidence.snippet: Autosomal-dominant adult-onset neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis (ANCL) is characterized by accumulation of autofluorescent storage material in neural tissues and neurodegeneration

readouts.interpretation: Increased autofluorescent storage material reports the core lysosomal lipopigment accumulation node.

evidence.reference_title: Kufs disease, the major adult form of neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis, caused by mutations in CLN6.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in CLN6 are the major cause of recessive Kufs type A disease.

evidence.reference_title: Kufs disease due to mutation of CLN6: clinical, pathological and molecular genetic features.
evidence.snippet: Mutation of CLN6 has emerged as the most important cause of recessive Kufs disease but, remarkably, is also responsible for variant late infantile ceroid lipofuscinosis.

nodes.description: CLN6 is an endoplasmic-reticulum-associated component of the CLN6-CLN8 EGRESS complex that recruits lysosomal enzymes at the ER and promotes their Golgi transfer. CLN6 deficiency impairs ER export of lysosomal enzymes and lowers lysosomal enzyme levels, providing a mechanistic lysosome-biogenesis branch for CLN6-related Kufs disease.
nodes.id: CLN6 EGRESS Complex Lysosomal Enzyme Trafficking Defect

biological_processes: endoplasmic reticulum to Golgi vesicle-mediated transport

evidence.reference_title: Kufs disease due to mutation of CLN6: clinical, pathological and molecular genetic features.
evidence.snippet: Mutation of CLN6 has emerged as the most important cause of recessive Kufs disease but, remarkably, is also responsible for variant late infantile ceroid lipofuscinosis.

evidence.reference_title: A CLN6-CLN8 complex recruits lysosomal enzymes at the ER for Golgi transfer.
evidence.snippet: Experiments focused on protein interaction and trafficking identified CLN6 as an obligate component of a CLN6-CLN8 complex (herein referred to as EGRESS: ER-to-Golgi relaying of enzymes of the lysosomal system), which recruits lysosomal enzymes at the ER to promote their Golgi transfer.

evidence.reference_title: A CLN6-CLN8 complex recruits lysosomal enzymes at the ER for Golgi transfer.
evidence.snippet: Mice lacking both CLN6 and CLN8 did not display aggravated pathology compared with the single deficiencies, indicating that the EGRESS complex works as a functional unit.

evidence.reference_title: Cathepsin F mutations cause Type B Kufs disease, an adult-onset neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis.
evidence.snippet: Exome sequencing of five samples from the two families identified homozygous and compound heterozygous missense mutations in CTSF within this linkage region.

evidence.reference_title: Cathepsin F mutations cause Type B Kufs disease, an adult-onset neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis.
evidence.snippet: CTSF encodes cathepsin F, a lysosomal cysteine protease, dysfunction of which is a highly plausible candidate mechanism for a storage disorder like ceroid lipofuscinosis.

nodes.description: Recessive missense variants in CTSF impair cathepsin F, a lysosomal cysteine protease, providing a plausible mechanism for the storage-disease phenotype of Type B Kufs disease. In silico modeling predicts altered protein structure and function, and a Ctsf knockout mouse recapitulates the light- and electron-microscopic pathology of Kufs disease.

evidence.reference_title: Cathepsin F mutations cause Type B Kufs disease, an adult-onset neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis.
evidence.snippet: CTSF encodes cathepsin F, a lysosomal cysteine protease, dysfunction of which is a highly plausible candidate mechanism for a storage disorder like ceroid lipofuscinosis.

evidence.reference_title: Cathepsin F mutations cause Type B Kufs disease, an adult-onset neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis.
evidence.snippet: re-examination of a previously published mouse knockout of Ctsf shows that it recapitulates the light and electron-microscopic pathological features of Kufs disease.

nodes.id: Cathepsin F lysosomal cysteine protease activity

evidence.reference_title: Cathepsin F mutations cause Type B Kufs disease, an adult-onset neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis.
evidence.snippet: CTSF encodes cathepsin F, a lysosomal cysteine protease, dysfunction of which is a highly plausible candidate mechanism for a storage disorder like ceroid lipofuscinosis.

readouts.interpretation: Decreased cathepsin F cysteine protease function reports the CTSF-driven lysosomal proteolysis defect.

nodes.description: Ataxia is the most prominent motor manifestation of CLN6-related Kufs disease and a typical motor feature of Type B Kufs disease.

evidence.reference_title: Kufs disease due to mutation of CLN6: clinical, pathological and molecular genetic features.
evidence.snippet: Ataxia was the most prominent motor feature.

nodes.description: Progressive cerebral and cerebellar atrophy is the typical neuroimaging finding in adult NCL.

evidence.reference_title: Kufs disease due to mutation of CLN6: clinical, pathological and molecular genetic features.
evidence.snippet: MRI showed progressive cerebral and cerebellar atrophy.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in DNAJC5, encoding cysteine-string protein alpha, cause autosomal-dominant adult-onset neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis.
evidence.snippet: we identified in five individuals one of two disease-causing mutations, c.346_348delCTC and c.344T>G, in DNAJC5 encoding cysteine-string protein alpha (CSPα).

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in DNAJC5, encoding cysteine-string protein alpha, cause autosomal-dominant adult-onset neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis.
evidence.snippet: These mutations-causing a deletion, p.Leu116del, and an amino acid exchange, p.Leu115Arg, respectively-are located within the cysteine-string domain of the protein and affect both palmitoylation-dependent sorting and the amount of CSPα in neuronal cells.

nodes.description: In autosomal dominant CLN4 (Kufs-A), pathogenic variants in DNAJC5 encoding cysteine-string protein alpha (CSPalpha) impair palmitoylation-dependent sorting and reduce the amount of CSPalpha in neuronal cells. Functional depletion of CSPalpha is proposed to drive presynaptic dysfunction and progressive neurodegeneration in parallel with lysosomal accumulation of misfolded, proteolysis-resistant proteins that form characteristic ceroid deposits in neurons.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in DNAJC5, encoding cysteine-string protein alpha, cause autosomal-dominant adult-onset neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis.
evidence.snippet: The resulting depletion of functional CSPα might cause in parallel the presynaptic dysfunction and the progressive neurodegeneration observed in affected individuals and lysosomal accumulation of misfolded and proteolysis-resistant proteins in the form of characteristic ceroid deposits in neurons.

nodes.description: Progressive dementia is a core manifestation of both Type A and Type B adult NCL and appears to be an invariable accompaniment in CLN6-related disease.

evidence.reference_title: Kufs disease due to mutation of CLN6: clinical, pathological and molecular genetic features.
evidence.snippet: Dementia appeared to be an invariable accompaniment, although it could take a number of years to manifest and occasionally cognitive impairment preceded myoclonic seizures.

nodes.description: Patients with CLN6-related Type A Kufs disease are usually highly photosensitive on EEG.

evidence.reference_title: Kufs disease due to mutation of CLN6: clinical, pathological and molecular genetic features.
evidence.snippet: Patients were usually highly photosensitive on EEG.

nodes.description: Adult NCL neurons accumulate autofluorescent ceroid lipopigment in lysosomal storage bodies. This is the defining pathological hallmark of Kufs disease and the broader NCL group.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in DNAJC5, encoding cysteine-string protein alpha, cause autosomal-dominant adult-onset neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis.
evidence.snippet: Autosomal-dominant adult-onset neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis (ANCL) is characterized by accumulation of autofluorescent storage material in neural tissues and neurodegeneration

evidence.reference_title: Kufs disease due to mutation of CLN6: clinical, pathological and molecular genetic features.
evidence.snippet: Ultrastructural examination of the pathology revealed fingerprint profiles as the characteristic inclusions, but they were not reliably seen in tissues other than brain.

evidence.reference_title: A CLN6-CLN8 complex recruits lysosomal enzymes at the ER for Golgi transfer.
evidence.snippet: In vitro and in vivo studies showed that CLN6 deficiency results in inefficient ER export of lysosomal enzymes and diminished levels of the enzymes at the lysosome.

evidence.reference_title: A CLN6-CLN8 complex recruits lysosomal enzymes at the ER for Golgi transfer.
evidence.snippet: In vitro and in vivo studies showed that CLN6 deficiency results in inefficient ER export of lysosomal enzymes and diminished levels of the enzymes at the lysosome.

readouts.interpretation: Lower lysosomal enzyme levels report impaired CLN6-dependent ER-to-Golgi lysosomal-enzyme transfer.

nodes.description: Myoclonic seizures are debilitating in Type A Kufs disease.

evidence.snippet: The typical presentation was of progressive myoclonus epilepsy with debilitating myoclonic seizures and relatively infrequent tonic-clonic seizures.

nodes.description: Adult NCL produces a progressive neurodegenerative phenotype manifesting as dementia, ataxia, and motor system dysfunction, with or without progressive myoclonus epilepsy depending on subtype.

evidence.snippet: lysosomal accumulation of misfolded and proteolysis-resistant proteins in the form of characteristic ceroid deposits in neurons.

readouts.interpretation: Proteolysis-resistant neuronal ceroid deposits report the lysosomal protein-aggregation consequence of DNAJC5/CSPalpha dysfunction.
These deposits are a DNAJC5-related manifestation of the broader adult NCL lysosomal lipopigment storage node.

nodes.description: Generalized tonic-clonic seizures are characteristic but relatively infrequent in Type A Kufs disease compared with myoclonic seizures.

evidence.reference_title: Kufs disease due to mutation of CLN6: clinical, pathological and molecular genetic features.
evidence.snippet: The typical presentation was of progressive myoclonus epilepsy with debilitating myoclonic seizures and relatively infrequent tonic-clonic seizures.

nodes.description: No disease-modifying therapy exists for adult NCL. Care is supportive and symptomatic, including management of cognitive decline, motor disability, and psychiatric comorbidity.

evidence.reference_title: Adult-onset neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis misdiagnosed as autoimmune encephalitis and normal-pressure hydrocephalus: A 10-year case report and case-based review.
evidence.snippet: Despite various treatments, the patient's condition did not improve.
